The plan is ready, but now it is about people. Every manager must own the message, and we have to build buy-in fast.
And the resistance? Some of the senior people are not happy with this change.
Resistance is normal. I do not have to win every argument, but I should listen, then explain the why again.
And how do you keep the energy up over six months?
We protect the momentum. People need to feel safe to escalate a problem, and we should celebrate every small win out loud.

Carlos, the integration is moving, but now you have to lead the people, not just the plan. Let me brief you on the change side. First, every manager must own the message; nobody can stay silent, because silence creates fear. Second, you have to be visible. People need to see the leaders, so you should walk the floor and answer hard questions in the open. Third, set clear expectations: the teams must hit the milestones, but they need to know it is safe to flag a problem early. We should reward the people who raise risks, not punish them. Fourth, resistance is normal. You do not have to win every argument; you have to listen, then explain the why one more time. Finally, protect the momentum. You must celebrate the small wins out loud, because a change that feels slow loses energy fast. Lead the change the same way you led the deal, but remember: this time the work is people, not numbers.

Hi Carlos, this is Diana from the board, with a quick recap after the town hall today. Overall, very strong. You were visible, you answered the hard questions directly, and the teams felt heard, which is exactly what a change like this needs. A few notes. First, the message: it is clear, but you have to repeat it more often; people only believe a change when they hear it many times. Second, the managers: most of them own the message now, but two are still silent, and you must coach them this week, because a quiet manager creates a nervous team. Third, the risk culture: keep rewarding the people who flag problems early; that is working well, so do not change it. One thing to fix: a few people think the milestones are optional, so make it clear that the dates are firm and the teams have to hit them. Send me your engagement plan by Friday, and we should review the momentum at the end of the month. Strong start on the people side, Carlos. Talk soon.

First, take the mandate from the board. Then set the expectations for the managers. Next, hold the town hall and answer the hard questions. After that, handle the resistance and build buy-in. Finally, protect the momentum with small wins.
You should walk the floor and answer the hard questions.
